claim: An open pseudo-3D Sleipner benchmark enables benchmark-constrained temporal support inference that improves field-facing support mapping on both p07 and p10.
methods_results_block:
Claim: An open pseudo-3D Sleipner benchmark enables benchmark-constrained temporal support inference that improves field-facing support mapping on both p07 and p10.

Methods: We keep the 2D PyTorch models and classical baselines, but the main field-facing result now centers on plain ML plus benchmark-constrained temporal structured support inference rather than the hybrid model. We evaluate the original structured reconstruction, the new temporal structured extension, and a layered structured negative control. Field outputs are benchmark-constrained using the public Sleipner storage interval, and the pseudo-3D evidence is reported on 11-inline p07 and p10 benchmarks.

Results: Across a three-seed sweep on the harder benchmark-v2 synthetic task, plain ML reaches mean Dice 0.206 ± 0.032 on the OOD split and 0.544 ± 0.008 on the test split. The hybrid model reaches 0.166 ± 0.024 on OOD and 0.494 ± 0.064 on test, while the strongest deterministic classical baseline (difference) reaches 0.108 Dice on the same OOD benchmark. This means the seed sweep does not support a hybrid-centered synthetic story. On the 11-inline p07 temporal benchmark, structured plain ML improves support-volume IoU from 0.300 to 0.438, and the temporal structured extension improves it further to 0.600. Trace-support IoU moves from 0.873 to 0.879 to 0.910, while crossline continuity moves from 0.967 to 0.982 to 0.986. On the direct 11-inline p10 benchmark, structured plain ML improves support-volume IoU from 0.562 to 0.685, and the temporal structured extension improves it further to 0.771. Lateral support alignment moves from 0.910 to 0.907 to 0.907, and the best constrained classical baseline reaches 0.363 support-volume IoU. The hybrid field outputs remain weaker on the direct benchmark (0.097 support-volume IoU), so the current defensible field claim should center on temporal structured support inference. Across the reused three-seed field evaluation, temporal structured plain ML becomes the strongest support-mapping variant on p10 with support-volume IoU 0.709 ± 0.044, compared with 0.637 ± 0.037 for the non-temporal structured baseline and 0.526 ± 0.048 for plain ML alone. On p07, structured plain ML remains stronger than plain ML on support-volume IoU (0.440 ± 0.052 versus 0.304 ± 0.061), and the temporal structured extension improves it further to 0.587 ± 0.025. This stability check therefore supports a method claim rather than only a benchmark/protocol claim. The layered extension remains a tested negative result rather than the field method of record.
